title: Docker clone
business_case_summary:
Business Case Summary: Docker clone (DEM-2026-0065)

Executive Summary
This project proposes creating an internal Docker clone to replicate its core containerization functionality. The objective is to establish an alternative, self-managed platform.

Direct Business Value/Impact
Developing an in-house Docker alternative could significantly reduce reliance on commercial licensing and external vendor roadmaps. This offers enhanced control and potential long-term cost efficiencies for our infrastructure.

Potential Risk Mitigation
Due to high risk, a comprehensive architectural design and a phased development strategy are essential. Thorough security reviews and leveraging open-source components will help mitigate technical complexities and ensure stability.
capacity_reasoning: High-risk demand requires Senior Architect oversight.
Delivery team has 22% overall available capacity.
Constraint: Security Engineer has only 0 units available (requires 15).
Constraint: QA Engineer has only 0 units available (requires 20).
Skill gap: Required skills Containerization, Linux Kernel Programming, Go, System Architecture, Networking, Storage, API Design, Distributed Systems are missing in the available workforce.
Earliest available start date is deferred by 30 days to 2026-08-08.
